title: Murraya japonensis
taxonomy: Murraya japonensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the orange jasmine. It is a member of the genus Murraya, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The Rutaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, including citrus and rue, among others. Murraya japonensis is often cultivated for its fragrant flowers and is also used in traditional medicine in some parts of Asia. The specific classification of Murraya japonensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Sapindales, Family: Rutaceae, Genus: Murraya, Species: M. paniculata, but the name is sometimes also referred to as Murraya japonensis.
uses: Murraya japonensis, also known as the Yellow Jessamine or Orange Jessamine, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, Murraya japonensis is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and skin infections. It is also believed to have antioxidant and antiviral properties, which can help boost the immune system. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Murraya japonensis for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Murraya japonensis is known to be relatively safe when used in moderation, but excessive consumption can lead to dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset. intro: Murraya japonensis, also known as Chinese boxwood or Japanese box, is a small evergreen tree or shrub native to China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Murraya japonensis include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used to make a topical cream or ointment for skin conditions and wounds.
